claim: India banned UPI payments for international transactions.
evidence: NPCI has actually expanded UPI to multiple countries like Singapore, UAE, and Mauritius. No ban has been announced.

claim: NASA discovered a parallel universe where time runs backwards.
evidence: NASA researchers found evidence of high-energy particles in Antarctica that could be explained by various theories, but a parallel universe was just one speculative hypothesis, not a confirmed discovery.

claim: WHO declared a new global health emergency for Monkeypox virus.
evidence: The World Health Organization (WHO) declared the upsurge of mpox in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and other countries a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC).
There is no scientific evidence that lemon water or any specific food can cure COVID-19. Vaccines and medical treatments are the only proven methods.

claim: The Indian government is providing free recharge for 3 months to all citizens.
evidence: This is a widespread scam message. The government has not announced any such scheme. Such links are often phishing attempts to steal personal data.

claim: Eating garlic can prevent infection with the new coronavirus.
evidence: Garlic is a healthy food that may have some antimicrobial properties. However, there is no evidence from the current outbreak that eating garlic has protected people from the new coronavirus.
Viruses cannot travel on radio waves/mobile networks. COVID-19 is spreading in many countries that do not have 5G mobile networks.

claim: The moon is made of green cheese.
evidence: The moon is composed of silicate rock and various minerals, as confirmed by geological samples brought back by Apollo missions.
